title: cotoneaster fontanesii var. nummularius
intro: Cotoneaster fontanesii var. nummularius, also known as a dwarf cotoneaster, is a compact shrub native to the Caucasus region, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and red berries. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Cotoneaster fontanesii var. nummularius is believed to offer several health benefits, including antioxidant, immune-boosting, and antiviral effects, which may help to prevent or alleviate various conditions such as infections and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and berries can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, often in combination with other herbs, to support overall health and well-being.
